byChelsea Hylton, KOMO News Staff

EDMONDS, Wash. —TheEdmonds Police Departmentarrested two people and is seeking a third after a three-week spree of shootings along Olympic View Drive.

Police arrested a 37-year-old man and a 26-year-old woman, both from Lynnwood for a drive-by shooting.

In early June, police received numerous calls from community members about sounds of gunshots around the 8100 block of Olympic View Drive. Police said most of the calls came in the late evening and overnight hours.

During their investigation police were able to find evidence showing that multiple different weapons were being used in the area. Some trees and a stop sign were shot.

On June 24, a resident called police to report that a gunshot had entered their home. During this time, authorities were surveilling the area to try and identify those responsible.



On June 27, multiple officers established a “dedicated surveillance” and saw a vehicle enter the area then shortly after they heard multiple gunshots. Police said they found the vehicle unoccupied in the residential area.

After a search with the assistance of two K9 units from Edmonds and Everett, King County’s Guardian 1 helicopter, and a drone unit they were able to locate two suspects. Police also recovered a handgun during their arrest.

A search warrant was served on the suspect vehicle where additional items of evidence were found.

Police are still investigating this incident and are searching for anyone else involved in the shootings.

If anyone has any information on these shootings they are asked to contact Edmonds police.

2025Sinclair, Inc.
title: Edmonds police arrest 2 people after several shootings along Olympic View Drive
description: The Edmonds Police Department arrested two people and is seeking a third after a three-week spree of shootings along Olympic View Drive.
